title: six6 Idea Node
description: One lightweight experimental Idea node that may share a graph with Memory nodes without inheriting Memory candidate, approval, or ingestion audit rules.

id.description: Stable Idea node identity inside the graph. This is independent from Memory candidate_id and Memory.id.

content.description: Raw or lightly shaped idea text. Noise and speculation are allowed.

created_at.description: Time when the idea node record was created.

observed_at.description: Optional source event time when it differs from created_at.

source.description: Where this idea came from. Idea sources may be informal and do not imply Memory audit approval.

ref.description: Logical source path, event id, issue key, or process name.

section.description: Optional source section, heading, or local anchor.

state.description: Lifecycle state for the idea lane. This is not a Memory approval state.

maturity.description: Idea maturity signal from noisy seed to ready-for-promotion candidate. It does not satisfy Memory maturity or approval requirements.

tags.description: Optional lightweight labels for clustering and browsing.

confidence.description: Optional weak confidence signal from 0.0 to 1.0.

$comment: Idea nodes are allowed to be experimental, noisy, and reversible. Promotion to Memory requires a separate Memory candidate and approved-decision flow; an idea-node.v1 record must never be treated as an approved Memory record.
